summary: Frontend-only regression test of the two morse decoder bug fixes. Generated synthetic SOS / noisy-SOS / PARIS WAVs at 700Hz and fed them via file input. Bug 1 ('reads E') is NOT fixed — it has merely shifted: now SOS decodes as a single 'T' (one continuous dash) regardless of threshold (tested 50/80/140/180). Bug 2 (broadband-noise triggering) also fails its assertion — noisy SOS decodes to empty string. The new bandpass + separate detector AnalyserNode architecture is correctly wired (visible filter peak/highlight on FFT, off-pitch test at 1000Hz correctly rejects 700Hz signal with only 'U' spurious — within tolerance), but the detector AnalyserNode's smoothingTimeConstant of 0.85 prevents the post-filter level from dropping below threshold during the ~80ms intra-symbol gaps, fusing the entire SOS message into one continuous ON state.

integration_issues.issue: decoded-text-output reads 'T' (single dash) instead of 'SOS'. Reproduced at thresholds 50, 80, 140, 180 — same 'T' result every time. Level stays clipped at 255 for the entire 2.2s of SOS playback, then decays slowly to 0 over ~1000ms AFTER audio ends, producing exactly one OFF edge → one dash → 'T'. auto-unit consequently clamps to max 300ms (WPM 4).

integration_issues.flow: Bug 2 regression — decoding noisy SOS @ 700Hz with broadband noise
integration_issues.issue: decoded-text-output is empty after playback. Likely same root cause: the detector level is dominated by the smoothing constant and stays high until audio ends; with the noise the OFF transition may happen earlier/later but no symbols are committed in this run.

passed_checks: All required data-testids present and stable
capture-status-badge correctly transitions IDLE → LIVE on file-play-btn click
fft-canvas and wave-canvas both render content during playback (FFT non-black pixel count 3890, wave 21874)
FFT shows 700Hz pitch marker and threshold reference line
Pitch slider keyboard interaction works (700 → 1000 Hz via ArrowRight on the [role=slider] thumb)
Off-pitch bandpass rejection: target=1000Hz, audio=700Hz → decoded 'U' (1-2 spurious chars, WITHIN the stated 1-2 char tolerance) — bandpass + separate detector wiring is functionally correct
Copy button: copies decoded text and shows 'Copied decoded text' sonner toast
No console errors during any of the playback runs

rca_of_the_issue.symptom: SOS @ 700Hz → 'T'; noisy SOS → ''. The detector never sees the gaps between dots/dashes.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In ensureAudioCtx() the new detector AnalyserNode is created with smoothingTimeConstant=0.85. With FFT bin updates ~every 23ms and renderLoop reads via requestAnimationFrame (~16.7ms), a clipped peak of 255 from the bandpass-resonant 700Hz tone smooths down at ~15% per frame. After an 80ms intra-symbol gap (~3-5 frames) the smoothed level is still ~0.85^5 = 44% of 255 ≈ 113, well above any usable threshold. Result: the entire SOS message is one continuous ON, and the only OFF edge occurs ~1 second after the audio file ends (visible in the 100ms-cadence level capture: level=255 from t=200ms to t=2400ms, then slow decay 226→0 between t=2400ms and t=3400ms).
rca_of_the_issue.fix_recommendation: Reduce detectorRef.smoothingTimeConstant to 0.1–0.3 (or 0). The bandpass filter already provides the frequency selectivity; the detector analyser should be high-temporal-resolution. Additionally consider widening the bandpass band (lower Q, e.g. Q=6-8) so that the post-filter envelope decays faster — though the Q=12 ringdown (~5.5ms) is actually fine; smoothing is the dominant decay term. Also consider raising MIN_EDGE_MS marginally only if false edges appear after the smoothing fix, and validating that auto-unit doesn't immediately clamp to max because the very first measured duration is the whole-file length.
reproduction_steps: Synthesize /tmp/sos.wav with the script from the request (700Hz, unit_ms=80, noise_amp=0)
Open the app, click tab-file, select the WAV via file-input, click file-play-btn
Repeating with threshold values 50, 80, 140, 180 all yield 'T'

action_items: Lower detectorRef.smoothingTimeConstant from 0.85 to ~0.1-0.3 (or 0) in ensureAudioCtx() inside /app/frontend/src/components/MorseDecoder.jsx
After the smoothing fix, retest SOS @ 700Hz — expect decoded to contain 'S' and 'O', wpm-readout > 0, unit-readout near 80ms
Retest noisy SOS — expect at minimum 'S' or the first dots of 'S'
Optional: cap the initial dotDurations sample so a single very long edge cannot clamp the unit to 300ms (e.g. ignore samples > 4*current unit until two consistent samples are seen)
critical_code_review_comments: detectorRef smoothingTimeConstant=0.85 is the dominant time constant in the edge-detection chain and is at odds with the goal of resolving 80ms dots — this is the active bug

auto-unit can be poisoned by a single long initial duration: dotDurations.push(dur/3 for dash) — if dur happens to be the whole SOS message (~2200ms), the unit instantly jumps to clamp(300) and never recovers within a 3s file. Consider rejecting outliers or seeding dotDurations only after the second confirmed edge

filterRef + detectorRef are created lazily in ensureAudioCtx but the file source connects to filterRef.current directly — if ensureAudioCtx wasn't called yet on first interaction the wiring would be undefined. Currently safe because playFile awaits ensureAudioCtx, but worth a guard

MIN_EDGE_MS = 12ms is fine, but with smoothing 0.85 the rising edge is also smoothed — combined latency may shift apparent durations. After lowering smoothing, recheck that dot classification (dur < 2*unit) still holds

success_rate.frontend: 60% — UI + off-pitch + visualizers + copy/clear pass; the core bug-fix regression for Bug 1 and Bug 2 fails

seed_data_creation: Generated /tmp/sos.wav, /tmp/sos_noisy.wav, /tmp/paris.wav via /tmp/synth.py (Python wave module) per the synthesis script in the request
context_for_next_testing_agent: The synthetic WAVs at /tmp/sos.wav etc. are still on disk and can be reused. After the main agent lowers the detector smoothingTimeConstant, retest the exact same flow: tab-file → file-input → file-play-btn → wait ~4s → read decoded-text-output. The test infrastructure (focusing [role=slider] thumb and using ArrowLeft/Right to adjust sliders) works reliably. The off-pitch test (set pitch to 1000 and play sos.wav) is a useful negative control — it should remain near-empty (≤2 chars) after the fix.
